page: Google Cloud Platform c4a-highmem-64 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance belonging to the c4a server family. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs built on the x86_64 CPU architecture, paired with 512 GB of system memory. This configuration provides a resource density of 8.0 GB of RAM per vCPU, designed for memory-intensive workloads that require substantial memory capacity relative to compute resources.

This server type does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, meaning all storage requirements must be met through network-attached block storage options. Additionally, it does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The absence of hardware accelerators and local storage represents a design tradeoff, prioritizing high-capacity memory and dedicated compute cores over local disk I/O and parallelized GPU processing.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c4a-highmem-64 offers a high-density memory profile within the c4a family, allowing organizations to run large-scale applications without the overhead of provisioning excess vCPUs simply to acquire sufficient RAM. This resource balance is suitable for memory-intensive workloads such as relational databases, in-memory caching layers, enterprise applications, and medium-scale data processing pipelines.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4a-highmem-64 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine within the c4a server family, configured with 64 dedicated vCPUs and 512 GB of RAM. This configuration establishes a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core, positioning it as a memory-optimized resource. The instance does not feature local storage or integrated GPUs, requiring network-attached storage for persistent data. This design tradeoff focuses resources on memory capacity and dedicated compute allocation. The qualitative cost efficiency of this instance lies in its high memory density, which allows memory-bound applications to scale without requiring unnecessary compute overhead. It is suited for workloads such as large databases, in-memory caches, and enterprise data processing.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4a-highmem-64 is an x86_64 virtual machine featuring 64 dedicated vCPUs and 512 GB of RAM, designed for memory-intensive workloads without local storage or GPUs.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4a-highmem-64 offers 64 dedicated vCPUs and 512 GB of RAM on x86_64 architecture for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring sixty-four dedicated cores and half a terabyte of system memory for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 512 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core
No local storage or integrated GPU accelerators included
Optimized for memory-intensive enterprise applications and databases
Requires network-attached storage for data persistence
